<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;

  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}

  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;

  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}

  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }

  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */

    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);

  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;

    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}

Harmonic functions

Solutions of $\Delta u = 0$ — averaging, rigidity, and the Dirichlet problem. The bridge between complex analysis, electrostatics, and elliptic PDE.

blocks.html:
1. Laplace's equation

A function $u\colon \Omega \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n \to \mathbb{R}$ of class $C^2$ is called harmonic when

$$\Delta u \;=\; \sum_{i=1}^n \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x_i^2} \;=\; 0.$$

This is the simplest elliptic PDE, and it shows up everywhere: the steady-state heat distribution on a body whose boundary temperature is fixed; the electrostatic potential outside the supports of charges; the gravitational potential outside a mass distribution; the velocity potential of an incompressible irrotational fluid.

In two dimensions there is a magic bridge to complex analysis: if $f = u + iv$ is holomorphic on an open $\Omega \subseteq \mathbb{C}$, then differentiating the Cauchy–Riemann equations $u_x = v_y$, $u_y = -v_x$ once more gives $u_{xx} + u_{yy} = v_{yx} - v_{xy} = 0$. Both the real and imaginary part of any holomorphic function are harmonic. Conversely, on a simply connected $\Omega$, every harmonic $u$ has a harmonic conjugate $v$ — the unique-up-to-constant real function making $u + iv$ holomorphic.

Examples. Some staple harmonic functions on $\mathbb{R}^2$: linear $ax + by + c$; the real and imaginary parts of $z^n$ (so $\mathrm{Re}\,z^2 = x^2 - y^2$, $\mathrm{Im}\,z^2 = 2xy$, etc.); $\log|z|$ on $\mathbb{R}^2 \setminus \{0\}$; and $\arg z$ on any simply connected slit domain. Linear combinations are harmonic too. Polynomial harmonic functions of degree $\le n$ form a finite-dimensional space — the spherical harmonics in disguise.

2. The mean value property

Here is the single property that distils what "harmonic" really means. If $u$ is harmonic on $\Omega$ and the closed ball $\overline{B(x_0, r)} \subset \Omega$, then

$$u(x_0) \;=\; \frac{1}{|\partial B(x_0,r)|}\int_{\partial B(x_0,r)} u\,dS \;=\; \frac{1}{|B(x_0,r)|}\int_{B(x_0,r)} u\,dV.$$

The value at the center equals the spherical average — and equivalently the volume average over the ball. Harmonic = balanced.

The proof is one application of the divergence theorem: differentiate the average $\overline u(r) = \tfrac{1}{|\partial B(x_0,r)|}\int_{\partial B(x_0,r)} u\,dS$ with respect to $r$, get $\overline u'(r) = \tfrac{r}{n} \cdot \overline{\Delta u}(r) = 0$, so the average is independent of $r$. Sending $r \to 0$ recovers $u(x_0)$.

Converse — Koebe's theorem. The MVP characterises harmonicity. If $u$ is continuous on $\Omega$ and satisfies $u(x) = $ ball average for every admissible ball, then $u$ is harmonic — and in particular $C^\infty$. So a single integral identity, applied at every scale, forces all derivatives to exist and a PDE to hold. This is the cleanest example of elliptic regularity.

Two consequences fall out for free:

(i) Smoothing. Convolution with a radial bump, or even just averaging on small balls, takes a continuous harmonic function to itself — but for a generic $u$ it strictly improves regularity. Iterated averaging is therefore a route to constructing harmonic approximations.

(ii) Real analyticity. Going further: every harmonic function on a connected open set is real-analytic. Knowing $u$ on a tiny open ball pins it down everywhere, exactly as for holomorphic functions.

3. The maximum principle

From the mean value property the maximum principle drops out almost for free. If $u$ takes a value at $x_0$ which is its supremum on $\Omega$, then $u(x_0) = $ the average over any small ball around $x_0$, but the average can equal the maximum only if $u$ is constant on that ball. A connectedness argument propagates the constancy.

Weak maximum principle. Let $u$ be harmonic on a bounded $\Omega$ and continuous on $\overline\Omega$. Then

$$\max_{\overline\Omega} u \;=\; \max_{\partial\Omega} u, \qquad \min_{\overline\Omega} u \;=\; \min_{\partial\Omega} u.$$

Strong maximum principle. If $u$ attains its supremum at any interior point of a connected $\Omega$, then $u$ is constant on $\Omega$.

The strong form sharpens the weak: not only does the boundary realise the extremes, the interior can never do so unless $u$ is trivial.

Uniqueness for the Dirichlet problem. Suppose $u_1, u_2$ are both harmonic on $\Omega$ and continuous up to $\partial\Omega$, with $u_1 = u_2$ on $\partial\Omega$. The difference $w = u_1 - u_2$ is harmonic and zero on $\partial\Omega$. The weak maximum principle, applied to $\pm w$, forces $|w| \le 0$ on $\overline\Omega$. So $u_1 = u_2$. Boundary values determine harmonic functions.

4. The Poisson kernel and the disk Dirichlet problem

For the unit disk $\mathbb{D} = \{|z| < 1\}$ the Dirichlet problem has an explicit, beautiful solution. Given continuous boundary data $g\colon \partial \mathbb{D} \to \mathbb{R}$, the unique harmonic extension is

$$u(re^{i\theta}) \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi}\int_0^{2\pi} P_r(\theta - \varphi)\,g(\varphi)\,d\varphi, \qquad P_r(\theta) \;=\; \frac{1 - r^2}{1 - 2r\cos\theta + r^2}.$$

The kernel $P_r$ is the Poisson kernel. Three facts make it useful:

(i) Positivity. $1 - 2r\cos\theta + r^2 = |1 - re^{i\theta}|^2 > 0$, so $P_r > 0$.
(ii) Mass one. $\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_0^{2\pi} P_r(\theta)\,d\theta = 1$ for every $r \in [0,1)$.
(iii) Concentration. As $r \to 1^-$, $P_r$ concentrates a unit mass at $\theta = 0$ — it is an approximate identity.

So $u = P_r * g$ is convolution against an approximate identity: the values of $u$ deep in the disk are weighted averages of $g$ over the whole circle, while values close to the boundary look at nearby boundary points only.

Boundary recovery. Convergence to the boundary data has different flavours depending on how regular $g$ is:
• If $g$ is continuous, $u(re^{i\theta}) \to g(\theta)$ uniformly as $r \to 1^-$.
• If $g \in L^p$ for $1 \le p < \infty$, the radial limit equals $g$ almost everywhere — this is Fatou's theorem, the cleanest example of a measure-theoretic boundary value.
The MVP is the special case $r = 0$: $u(0) = \frac{1}{2\pi}\int g$, the simplest boundary integral.

5. Harnack's inequality

The Poisson formula gives an uniform control on the oscillation of positive harmonic functions. Suppose $u \ge 0$ is harmonic on $B(0, R)$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$. Then for every $|x| = r < R$,

$$\frac{R - r}{R + r}\cdot\Big(\frac{R}{R+r}\Big)^{\!n-2} u(0) \;\le\; u(x) \;\le\; \frac{R + r}{R - r}\cdot\Big(\frac{R}{R-r}\Big)^{\!n-2} u(0).$$

In two dimensions this collapses to the textbook form

$$\frac{R - r}{R + r}\,u(0) \;\le\; u(x) \;\le\; \frac{R + r}{R - r}\,u(0).$$

The proof on the disk is one line from the Poisson kernel. For $|y| = R$ and $|x| = r$,

$$P_{r/R}(\theta) \cdot \frac{1}{R^{n-2}} \;\le\; \text{Poisson kernel}(x, y) \;\le\; \text{(upper)},$$

and the positivity of $u$ on the boundary lets us pull the bound through the integral. Without positivity the bound is meaningless: the Poisson kernel at one point can be huge, and a sign-changing $u$ can have $u(0) = 0$ while $u$ is large elsewhere.

Liouville for harmonic functions. If $u \ge 0$ is harmonic on all of $\mathbb{R}^n$, apply Harnack on $B(0, R)$ with $r$ fixed and let $R \to \infty$. The factor $\tfrac{R-r}{R+r}$ tends to $1$, and the upper bound's $\tfrac{R+r}{R-r}$ also tends to $1$. Both bounds pinch to $u(0)$, so $u(x) = u(0)$ for every $x$ — a positive harmonic function on $\mathbb{R}^n$ is constant. The complex-analysis Liouville (bounded entire ⇒ constant) is the analogous statement for holomorphic functions; both are theorems about rigidity from a one-sided bound.

6. Subharmonic functions and Perron's method

Replace $\Delta v = 0$ with the inequality $\Delta v \ge 0$ — equivalently, replace the MVP equation $v(x) = $ ball average by the inequality $v(x) \le $ ball average. The resulting class of upper semicontinuous functions is the class of subharmonic functions. (Sign convention is a coin-flip; we follow the standard "sub" = below convention, so $v$ is subharmonic when its values sit below the harmonic majorant.)

Examples:

• If $f$ is holomorphic, $\log|f|$ is subharmonic — the elementary proof is the Jensen formula.
• Any convex function of a harmonic function is subharmonic (Jensen's inequality at the level of averages).
• Maxima of finitely many subharmonic functions are subharmonic.

The maximum principle still holds for subharmonic functions: $\sup_{\overline\Omega} v = \sup_{\partial\Omega} v$. This is the property that makes them useful — they let us build harmonic functions from below.

Perron's method

Given continuous boundary data $g$ on $\partial \Omega$, define the Perron family

$$\mathcal{S}_g \;=\; \{v\colon\Omega\to\mathbb{R} \;:\; v \text{ subharmonic},\ \limsup_{y\to\zeta} v(y) \le g(\zeta)\ \forall\zeta\in\partial\Omega\}.$$

and set

$$u(x) \;=\; \sup\,\{v(x) \;:\; v \in \mathcal{S}_g\}.$$

Two near-miraculous facts:

(i) $u$ is harmonic on $\Omega$. The proof is "harmonic lifting": on any small ball $B \subset\Omega$, replace $v$ by its Poisson integral on $B$, get a larger element of $\mathcal{S}_g$, and conclude that $u$ equals the Poisson integral of itself on every small ball.
(ii) $u$ achieves $g$ on $\partial\Omega$ at every regular boundary point. A boundary point $\zeta$ is regular when there is a barrier — a subharmonic function near $\zeta$ that vanishes at $\zeta$ and is strictly negative everywhere else. For "nice" domains (e.g. bounded with $C^1$ boundary in $\mathbb{R}^n$, or simply connected planar domains with more than one boundary point) every boundary point is regular.

The classical bad point: Lebesgue spine. In $\mathbb{R}^3$ remove a thin spike pointing into a region. The tip of the spike is a boundary point that admits no barrier — Perron's $u$ exists, but it does not achieve the boundary value at the tip. Pathologies of this kind delineate the boundary regularity theory and motivate Wiener's criterion.

(function(){

  if(!svg) return;

    x2y:     {u:(x,y)=>x*x*y,             lap:(x,y)=>2*y,               lbl:"x² y",            delLbl:"Δu = 2y"},
    exp:     {u:(x,y)=>Math.exp(x)*Math.cos(y), lap:(x,y)=>0,           lbl:"eˣ cos y",        delLbl:"Δu = 0  ✓ harmonic"},

    re_z3:   {u:(x,y)=>x*x*x-3*x*y*y,     lap:(x,y)=>0,                 lbl:"x³ − 3xy²",       delLbl:"Δu = 0  ✓ harmonic"}
  };
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const sel = document.getElementById("lap-fn").value;
    const cur = fns[sel];
    const N = 36;
    const cellW = 280/N, cellH = 280/N;
    const xMin=-2, xMax=2, yMin=-2, yMax=2;
    let uMin=Infinity, uMax=-Infinity, lMin=Infinity, lMax=-Infinity;
    const grid = [];
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const y = yMax - (j+0.5)*(yMax-yMin)/N;
      const row=[];
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const x = xMin + (i+0.5)*(xMax-xMin)/N;
        const u = cur.u(x,y), l = cur.lap(x,y);
        if(isFinite(u)){ uMin=Math.min(uMin,u); uMax=Math.max(uMax,u); }
        if(isFinite(l)){ lMin=Math.min(lMin,l); lMax=Math.max(lMax,l); }
        row.push({u,l});
      }
      grid.push(row);
    }
    const uAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(uMin), Math.abs(uMax), 1e-3);
    const lAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(lMin), Math.abs(lMax), 1e-3);
    // left panel: u
    const xLeft = 60;
    const xRight = 420;
    const top = 20;
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const cell = grid[j][i];
        const tu = cell.u/uAbs;
        const tl = cell.l/lAbs;
        if(isFinite(cell.u)){
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:xLeft+i*cellW, y:top+j*cellH, width:cellW+0.6, height:cellH+0.6,
            fill:heatColor(tu)}));
        }
        if(isFinite(cell.l)){
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:xRight+i*cellW, y:top+j*cellH, width:cellW+0.6, height:cellH+0.6,
            fill:heatColor(tl)}));
        }
      }
    }
    // borders + axes labels
    for(const [xx, ttl] of [[xLeft, "u(x,y) = "+cur.lbl], [xRight, "Δu(x,y)"]]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:xx, y:top, width:280, height:280, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-width":1}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:xx+140, y:top+296, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":13, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = ttl;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // axis ticks
    for(let xx of [xLeft, xRight]){
      const yA = top+280;
      const labels = [-2,-1,0,1,2];
      for(const v of labels){
        const px = xx + (v-xMin)/(xMax-xMin)*280;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px, y1:yA, x2:px, y2:yA+4, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      }
    }
    // colorbar legend
    const cbX = 720, cbY = top+10, cbH = 260, cbW = 14;
    for(let k=0;k<60;k++){
      const t = 1 - 2*k/59;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:cbX, y:cbY+k*(cbH/60), width:cbW, height:cbH/60+0.6, fill:heatColor(t)}));
    }
    const tHi = SVG("text",{x:cbX+7, y:cbY-4, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tHi.textContent = "+max"; svg.appendChild(tHi);
    const t0 = SVG("text",{x:cbX+7, y:cbY+cbH/2+3, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    t0.textContent = "0"; svg.appendChild(t0);
    const tLo = SVG("text",{x:cbX+7, y:cbY+cbH+12, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tLo.textContent = "−max"; svg.appendChild(tLo);

    document.getElementById("lap-readout").textContent =
      cur.delLbl + " · range over [-2,2]²: u ∈ ["+uMin.toFixed(2)+", "+uMax.toFixed(2)+"], Δu ∈ ["+lMin.toFixed(2)+", "+lMax.toFixed(2)+"]";
  }
  document.getElementById("lap-fn").addEventListener("change", draw);
  draw();
})();

/* ---------- Section 2: MVP center vs. circle average ---------- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("mvp-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const fns = {
    harm:   {u:(x,y)=>x*x-y*y,         lbl:"x² − y²",     harm:true},
    re_z3:  {u:(x,y)=>x*x*x-3*x*y*y,   lbl:"x³ − 3xy²",   harm:true},
    non1:   {u:(x,y)=>x*x+y*y,         lbl:"x² + y²",     harm:false},
    non2:   {u:(x,y)=>x*x*y,           lbl:"x² y",        harm:false}
  };
  let cx = 0.5, cy = 0.3;
  let dragging = false;
  function plot(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const fn = fns[document.getElementById("mvp-fn").value];
    const r  = parseFloat(document.getElementById("mvp-r").value);
    document.getElementById("mvp-rlabel").textContent = r.toFixed(2);
    const xMin=-3, xMax=3, yMin=-3, yMax=3;
    const W=300, H=300, padX=40, padY=10;
    function px(x){return padX + (x-xMin)/(xMax-xMin)*W;}
    function py(y){return padY + (yMax-y)/(yMax-yMin)*H;}
    // heatmap of u
    const N = 60;
    let uMin=Infinity, uMax=-Infinity;
    const cells = [];
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const y = yMax - (j+0.5)*(yMax-yMin)/N;
      const row=[];
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const x = xMin + (i+0.5)*(xMax-xMin)/N;
        const u = fn.u(x,y);
        uMin=Math.min(uMin,u); uMax=Math.max(uMax,u);
        row.push(u);
      }
      cells.push(row);
    }
    const uAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(uMin), Math.abs(uMax), 1e-3);
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const x0 = xMin + i*(xMax-xMin)/N;
        const x1 = xMin + (i+1)*(xMax-xMin)/N;
        const y0 = yMax - j*(yMax-yMin)/N;
        const y1 = yMax - (j+1)*(yMax-yMin)/N;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px(x0), y:py(y0), width:px(x1)-px(x0)+0.6, height:py(y1)-py(y0)+0.6,
          fill:heatColor(cells[j][i]/uAbs)}));
      }
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padX, y:padY, width:W, height:H, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // sample circle
    const samples = 200;
    let sum = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<samples;k++){
      const t = 2*Math.PI*k/samples;
      sum += fn.u(cx+r*Math.cos(t), cy+r*Math.sin(t));
    }
    const avg = sum/samples;
    const center = fn.u(cx, cy);
    // draw circle
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px(cx), cy:py(cy), r:Math.abs(px(cx+r)-px(cx)),
      fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px(cx), cy:py(cy), r:5, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    // info panel right
    const ix = padX + W + 30;
    const labels = [
      "u = " + fn.lbl,
      "harmonic? " + (fn.harm?"YES":"no"),
      "",
      "center  u(x₀)        = " + center.toFixed(3),
      "circle average      = " + avg.toFixed(3),
      "difference          = " + (avg - center).toFixed(4)
    ];
    let yy = padY + 30;
    for(const s of labels){
      const t = SVG("text",{x:ix, y:yy, "font-size":13, fill:s.startsWith("difference")?(fn.harm?"var(--green)":"var(--pink)"):"var(--ink)", "font-family":"ui-monospace,monospace"});
      t.textContent = s; svg.appendChild(t);
      yy += 18;
    }
    // verdict
    const verdict = fn.harm ? "MVP holds: avg = u(x₀) (up to discretisation)" : "MVP fails: avg ≠ u(x₀) — not harmonic";
    const tv = SVG("text",{x:ix, y:yy+14, "font-size":12, fill:fn.harm?"var(--green)":"var(--pink)", "font-style":"italic"});
    tv.textContent = verdict; svg.appendChild(tv);
    // hint text
    const th = SVG("text",{x:padX, y:padY+H+22, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    th.textContent = "drag the yellow center · slide r"; svg.appendChild(th);
  }
  function svgPoint(evt){
    const rect = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const xMin=-3, xMax=3, yMin=-3, yMax=3;
    const W=300, H=300, padX=40, padY=10;
    const sx = (evt.clientX-rect.left)*(svg.viewBox.baseVal.width/rect.width);
    const sy = (evt.clientY-rect.top )*(svg.viewBox.baseVal.height/rect.height);
    const x = xMin + (sx-padX)/W*(xMax-xMin);
    const y = yMax - (sy-padY)/H*(yMax-yMin);
    return [x,y];
  }
  svg.addEventListener("pointerdown", e=>{ dragging=true; const [x,y]=svgPoint(e); cx=x; cy=y; plot(); });
  svg.addEventListener("pointermove", e=>{ if(!dragging) return; const [x,y]=svgPoint(e); cx=x; cy=y; plot(); });
  window.addEventListener("pointerup", ()=>{ dragging=false; });
  document.getElementById("mvp-fn").addEventListener("change", plot);
  document.getElementById("mvp-r").addEventListener("input", plot);
  plot();
})();

    // domain circle
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px(0), cy:py(0), r:Math.abs(px(R)-px(0)),
      fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // find boundary max + interior max (numerically)
    let bMax=-Infinity, bx=0, by=0, iMax=-Infinity, ix=0, iy=0;
    for(let k=0;k<360;k++){
      const t = 2*Math.PI*k/360;
      const x = R*Math.cos(t), y = R*Math.sin(t);
      const u = fn.u(x,y);
      if(u>bMax){ bMax=u; bx=x; by=y; }
    }
    const M = 80;
    for(let j=0;j<M;j++){
      for(let i=0;i<M;i++){
        const x = -R + 2*R*(i+0.5)/M, y = -R + 2*R*(j+0.5)/M;
        if(x*x+y*y <= R*R*0.97){
          const u = fn.u(x,y);
          if(u>iMax){ iMax=u; ix=x; iy=y; }
        }
      }
    }
    // mark max points
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px(bx), cy:py(by), r:7, fill:"var(--yellow)", stroke:"#111", "stroke-width":1.5}));
    const tb = SVG("text",{x:px(bx)+12, y:py(by)+4, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    tb.textContent = "boundary max"; svg.appendChild(tb);
    if(Math.abs(iMax-bMax) > 1e-3 && (ix*ix+iy*iy) < R*R*0.85){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px(ix), cy:py(iy), r:7, fill:"var(--pink)", stroke:"#111", "stroke-width":1.5}));
      const ti = SVG("text",{x:px(ix)+12, y:py(iy)+4, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--pink)"});
      ti.textContent = "interior max!"; svg.appendChild(ti);
    }
    // summary
    const sx = padX+W+30;
    const lines = [
      "u = " + fn.lbl,
      "harmonic? " + (fn.harm?"YES":"no"),
      "",
      "max on ∂B(0,R) = " + bMax.toFixed(3),
      "max on B(0,R)  = " + iMax.toFixed(3),
      ""
    ];
    let yy = padY+20;
    for(const s of lines){
      const t = SVG("text",{x:sx, y:yy, "font-size":13, fill:"var(--ink)", "font-family":"ui-monospace,monospace"});
      t.textContent = s; svg.appendChild(t);
      yy += 18;
    }
    const verdict = fn.harm
      ? "Max attained on boundary  ✓  (max principle)"
      : "Interior max strictly above boundary — non-harmonic";
    const tv = SVG("text",{x:sx, y:yy+8, "font-size":12, fill:fn.harm?"var(--green)":"var(--pink)", "font-style":"italic"});
    tv.textContent = verdict; svg.appendChild(tv);
  }
  document.getElementById("max-fn").addEventListener("change", plot);
  document.getElementById("max-r").addEventListener("input", plot);
  plot();
})();

/* ---------- Section 4: Poisson kernel disk extension ---------- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("poi-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const datasets = {
    cos:   th=>Math.cos(th),
    cos2:  th=>Math.cos(2*th),
    step:  th=>(Math.sin(th)>0?1:-1),
    bump:  th=>Math.exp(-Math.pow((((th+Math.PI)%(2*Math.PI))-Math.PI)/0.5, 2)),
    "3cos2": th=>2 + 3*Math.cos(2*th)
  };
  function poisson(r, theta, g){
    const N = 360;
    let s = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const phi = 2*Math.PI*k/N;
      const Pr = (1 - r*r)/(1 - 2*r*Math.cos(theta-phi) + r*r);
      s += Pr * g(phi);
    }
    return s/N;
  }
  function plot(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const g = datasets[document.getElementById("poi-data").value];
    const r0 = parseFloat(document.getElementById("poi-r").value);
    document.getElementById("poi-rlabel").textContent = r0.toFixed(2);
    // left: disk heatmap of u
    const cx = 180, cy = 180, R = 150;
    // sample boundary range
    let gMin=Infinity,gMax=-Infinity;
    for(let k=0;k<400;k++){ const v=g(2*Math.PI*k/400); gMin=Math.min(gMin,v); gMax=Math.max(gMax,v); }
    const gAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(gMin), Math.abs(gMax), 1e-3);
    const N = 50;
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const py = 1 - 2*(j+0.5)/N;
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const px = -1 + 2*(i+0.5)/N;
        const r = Math.hypot(px,py);
        if(r >= 0.985) continue;
        const th = Math.atan2(py, px);
        const u = poisson(r, th, g);
        const X = cx + px*R, Y = cy - py*R;
        const dx = (2/N)*R*0.55;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:X-dx, y:Y-dx, width:2*dx+0.6, height:2*dx+0.6,
          fill:heatColor(u/gAbs)}));
      }
    }
    // disk boundary
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-width":1}));
    // boundary data colored ring
    const M = 120;
    for(let k=0;k<M;k++){
      const t1 = 2*Math.PI*k/M, t2 = 2*Math.PI*(k+1)/M;
      const tm = (t1+t2)/2;
      const v = g(tm)/gAbs;
      const x1 = cx+R*Math.cos(t1), y1 = cy-R*Math.sin(t1);
      const x2 = cx+(R+12)*Math.cos(t1), y2 = cy-(R+12)*Math.sin(t1);
      const x3 = cx+(R+12)*Math.cos(t2), y3 = cy-(R+12)*Math.sin(t2);
      const x4 = cx+R*Math.cos(t2), y4 = cy-R*Math.sin(t2);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:`M${x1} ${y1} L${x2} ${y2} A12 12 0 0 0 ${x3} ${y3} L${x4} ${y4} Z`,
        fill:heatColor(v), stroke:"none"}));
    }
    // radial slice circle
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R*r0, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // labels
    const tL = SVG("text",{x:cx, y:cy-R-22, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":13, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tL.textContent = "u(re^{iθ}) on disk"; svg.appendChild(tL);

    // right: radial slice u(r₀ e^{iθ}) vs θ (and boundary g)
    const px0 = 380, py0 = 30, pW = 320, pH = 280;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0, y:py0, width:pW, height:pH, fill:"#0b0f16", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // axes
    const yMid = py0+pH/2;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px0, y1:yMid, x2:px0+pW, y2:yMid, stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // tick markers θ ∈ [0, 2π]
    for(let k=0;k<=4;k++){
      const x = px0 + pW*k/4;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x, y1:py0+pH-2, x2:x, y2:py0+pH+4, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x, y:py0+pH+16, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = ["0","π/2","π","3π/2","2π"][k];
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // plot boundary g and slice
    const samp = 200;
    let dG = "M", dU = "M";
    for(let k=0;k<=samp;k++){
      const th = 2*Math.PI*k/samp;
      const gv = g(th)/gAbs;
      const uv = poisson(r0, th, g)/gAbs;
      const X = px0 + pW*k/samp;
      const Yg = yMid - gv*(pH/2-10);
      const Yu = yMid - uv*(pH/2-10);
      dG += (k===0?"":"L") + X + " " + Yg + " ";
      dU += (k===0?"":"L") + X + " " + Yu + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dG, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--mute)", "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dU, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2}));
    const lg = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:py0+18, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lg.textContent = "boundary g(θ)"; svg.appendChild(lg);
    const lu = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:py0+34, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    lu.textContent = "u at radius r = " + r0.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(lu);
    // u(0) by mvp
    const uCenter = poisson(0, 0, g);
    const tc = SVG("text",{x:px0+pW-8, y:py0+18, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    tc.textContent = "u(0) = avg(g) = " + uCenter.toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(tc);

    document.getElementById("poi-readout").textContent =
      "At r = "+r0.toFixed(2)+", the slice is the convolution P_r * g. As r → 1, P_r concentrates at θ = 0 and the yellow curve overlays the dashed boundary; as r → 0, the slice flattens to the constant u(0) = "+uCenter.toFixed(3)+".";
  }
  document.getElementById("poi-data").addEventListener("change", plot);
  document.getElementById("poi-r").addEventListener("input", plot);
  plot();
})();

/* ---------- Section 5: Harnack corridor ---------- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("har-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const datasets = {
    uniform:  th=>1,
    bump1:    th=>1 + 4*Math.exp(-Math.pow((((th+Math.PI)%(2*Math.PI))-Math.PI)/0.6, 2)),
    bump2:    th=>1 + 12*Math.exp(-Math.pow((((th+Math.PI)%(2*Math.PI))-Math.PI)/0.2, 2)),
    two:      th=>1 + 4*Math.exp(-Math.pow((((th+Math.PI)%(2*Math.PI))-Math.PI)/0.4, 2))
                    + 4*Math.exp(-Math.pow((((th)%(2*Math.PI))-Math.PI)/0.4, 2))
  };
  function poisson(r, theta, g){
    const N = 400;
    let s = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const phi = 2*Math.PI*k/N;
      const Pr = (1 - r*r)/(1 - 2*r*Math.cos(theta-phi) + r*r);
      s += Pr * g(phi);
    }
    return s/N;
  }
  function plot(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const g = datasets[document.getElementById("har-data").value];
    const r = parseFloat(document.getElementById("har-r").value);
    document.getElementById("har-rlabel").textContent = r.toFixed(2);
    // left: disk + sample circle of radius r where u is sampled
    const cx = 180, cy = 180, R = 150;
    let gMin=Infinity,gMax=-Infinity;
    for(let k=0;k<400;k++){ const v=g(2*Math.PI*k/400); gMin=Math.min(gMin,v); gMax=Math.max(gMax,v); }
    const gAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(gMin), Math.abs(gMax), 1e-3);
    const N = 44;
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const py = 1 - 2*(j+0.5)/N;
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const px = -1 + 2*(i+0.5)/N;
        const rr = Math.hypot(px,py);
        if(rr >= 0.985) continue;
        const th = Math.atan2(py, px);
        const u = poisson(rr, th, g);
        const X = cx + px*R, Y = cy - py*R;
        const dx = (2/N)*R*0.55;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:X-dx, y:Y-dx, width:2*dx+0.6, height:2*dx+0.6,
          fill:heatColor(u/gAbs)}));
      }
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R*r, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2, "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // sample u at center and on circle of radius r
    const u0 = poisson(0, 0, g);
    let uSampMin=Infinity, uSampMax=-Infinity;
    for(let k=0;k<200;k++){
      const th = 2*Math.PI*k/200;
      const uu = poisson(r, th, g);
      uSampMin = Math.min(uSampMin, uu);
      uSampMax = Math.max(uSampMax, uu);
    }
    // Harnack bounds (n=2)
    const lo = (1 - r)/(1 + r) * u0;
    const hi = (1 + r)/(1 - r) * u0;
    // right: bar graph
    const px0 = 380, py0 = 30, pW = 320, pH = 280;
    const yMax = Math.max(hi*1.05, uSampMax*1.05, 5);
    function bary(v){ return py0 + pH - (v/yMax)*pH; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0, y:py0, width:pW, height:pH, fill:"#0b0f16", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // Harnack corridor band
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0+30, y:bary(hi), width:pW-60, height:bary(lo)-bary(hi),
      fill:"var(--cyan)", opacity:0.15}));
    // bounds lines
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px0+30, y1:bary(lo), x2:px0+pW-30, y2:bary(lo), stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":2, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px0+30, y1:bary(hi), x2:px0+pW-30, y2:bary(hi), stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":2, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    // u(0) line
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px0+30, y1:bary(u0), x2:px0+pW-30, y2:bary(u0), stroke:"var(--violet)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // sample range bracket
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0+pW/2-20, y:bary(uSampMax), width:40, height:bary(uSampMin)-bary(uSampMax),
      fill:"var(--yellow)", opacity:0.85}));
    // labels
    const yL = py0+10;
    const lt = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:yL, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"}); lt.textContent = "Harnack corridor"; svg.appendChild(lt);
    const lv = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:yL+16, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--violet)"}); lv.textContent = "u(0)"; svg.appendChild(lv);
    const ly = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:yL+32, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--yellow)"}); ly.textContent = "actual range of u on |x|=r"; svg.appendChild(ly);
    // numerics
    let yy = py0+pH-100;
    const lines = [
      "u(0)             = " + u0.toFixed(3),
      "lower Harnack    = " + lo.toFixed(3),
      "upper Harnack    = " + hi.toFixed(3),
      "min u on |x|=r   = " + uSampMin.toFixed(3),
      "max u on |x|=r   = " + uSampMax.toFixed(3)
    ];
    for(const s of lines){
      const t = SVG("text",{x:px0+pW-8, y:yy, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--ink)", "font-family":"ui-monospace,monospace"});
      t.textContent = s; svg.appendChild(t);
      yy += 16;
    }
    const ok = (uSampMin >= lo - 1e-6) && (uSampMax <= hi + 1e-6);
    const tv = SVG("text",{x:px0+pW-8, y:py0+pH+18, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":12,
      fill:ok?"var(--green)":"var(--pink)", "font-style":"italic"});
    tv.textContent = ok ? "actual range ⊆ Harnack corridor  ✓" : "anomaly: corridor breached"; svg.appendChild(tv);
    document.getElementById("har-readout").textContent =
      "Bound: ("+((1-r)/(1+r)).toFixed(3)+") u(0) ≤ u(x) ≤ ("+((1+r)/(1-r)).toFixed(3)+") u(0). As r→0 the corridor collapses to {u(0)}; as r→1 it widens unboundedly.";
  }
  document.getElementById("har-data").addEventListener("change", plot);
  document.getElementById("har-r").addEventListener("input", plot);
  plot();
})();

/* ---------- Section 6: Perron supremum ---------- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("per-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const datasets = {
    cos:   th=>Math.cos(th),
    cos2:  th=>Math.cos(2*th),
    step:  th=>(Math.sin(th)>0?1:-1)
  };
  function poisson(r, theta, g){
    const N = 200;
    let s = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const phi = 2*Math.PI*k/N;
      const Pr = (1 - r*r)/(1 - 2*r*Math.cos(theta-phi) + r*r);
      s += Pr * g(phi);
    }
    return s/N;
  }
  // a "subharmonic competitor": cone tip at θ_k on the boundary, value g(θ_k); subharmonic on disk by max of linear functions
  function buildSub(family, x, y){
    let best = -Infinity;
    for(const c of family){
      // candidate v(z) = g(θ_k) − M*|z − e^{iθ_k}|^2 (a subharmonic paraboloid below the boundary value at one point)
      const dx = x - Math.cos(c.th), dy = y - Math.sin(c.th);
      const d2 = dx*dx + dy*dy;
      const v = c.val - c.M*d2;
      if(v>best) best=v;
    }
    return best;
  }
  function plot(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const g = datasets[document.getElementById("per-data").value];
    const n = parseInt(document.getElementById("per-n").value, 10);
    document.getElementById("per-nlabel").textContent = String(n);
    // sample boundary range
    let gMin=Infinity,gMax=-Infinity;
    for(let k=0;k<400;k++){ const v=g(2*Math.PI*k/400); gMin=Math.min(gMin,v); gMax=Math.max(gMax,v); }
    const gAbs = Math.max(Math.abs(gMin), Math.abs(gMax), 1e-3);
    // build family: n equally spaced θ_k, each contributes a subharmonic paraboloid v_k = g(θ_k) - M|z-e^{iθ_k}|²
    const family = [];
    const M = 1.5;
    for(let k=0;k<n;k++){
      const th = 2*Math.PI*k/n;
      family.push({th, val:g(th), M});
    }
    // diametral plot u along the real diameter [-1,1]
    const px0 = 50, py0 = 30, pW = 300, pH = 240;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0, y:py0, width:pW, height:pH, fill:"#0b0f16", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    const yMid = py0+pH/2;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px0, y1:yMid, x2:px0+pW, y2:yMid, stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // exact harmonic Poisson extension on diameter (along the x-axis, θ = 0 / π)
    const samp = 200;
    let dExact = "M", dPerron = "M";
    for(let k=0;k<=samp;k++){
      const x = -0.99 + 1.98*k/samp;
      // Poisson at point (x,0)
      const r = Math.abs(x), th = (x>=0?0:Math.PI);
      const ue = poisson(r, th, g)/gAbs;
      const us = buildSub(family, x, 0)/gAbs;
      const X = px0 + pW*k/samp;
      const Ye = yMid - ue*(pH/2-10);
      const Ys = yMid - us*(pH/2-10);
      dExact += (k===0?"":"L") + X + " " + Ye + " ";
      dPerron += (k===0?"":"L") + X + " " + Ys + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dExact, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":2}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dPerron, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":1.8, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    // labels
    const tg = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:py0+16, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"}); tg.textContent = "harmonic Poisson extension u(x,0)"; svg.appendChild(tg);
    const tp = SVG("text",{x:px0+8, y:py0+32, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--yellow)"}); tp.textContent = "Perron sup of "+n+" subharmonic competitors"; svg.appendChild(tp);
    // x ticks
    for(const v of [-1,-0.5,0,0.5,1]){
      const X = px0 + pW*(v+1)/2;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:X, y1:py0+pH-2, x2:X, y2:py0+pH+4, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const tt = SVG("text",{x:X, y:py0+pH+16, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      tt.textContent = String(v); svg.appendChild(tt);
    }
    // disk picture on right showing competitor locations
    const cx = 530, cy = 170, R = 110;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    for(const c of family){
      const X = cx + R*Math.cos(c.th), Y = cy - R*Math.sin(c.th);
      const v = c.val/gAbs;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:X, cy:Y, r:5, fill:heatColor(v), stroke:"var(--ink)", "stroke-width":1}));
    }
    const td = SVG("text",{x:cx, y:cy-R-12, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    td.textContent = "competitor pegs on ∂D"; svg.appendChild(td);
    document.getElementById("per-readout").textContent =
      "As n grows, the Perron supremum (yellow) climbs toward the harmonic Poisson extension (cyan). For continuous g and a regular domain, equality holds in the limit — that's Perron's theorem.";
  }
  document.getElementById("per-data").addEventListener("change", plot);
  document.getElementById("per-n").addEventListener("input", plot);
  plot();
})();
</script>

7. Connections

Harmonic functions are the genus-zero case of every theorem about elliptic and parabolic PDEs — once mean-value, maximum, and Harnack are internalised, the analysis of broader linear and nonlinear equations becomes incremental.

Complex analysis

The real and imaginary parts of a holomorphic function are conjugate harmonic functions; analyticity and the Cauchy–Riemann equations are the genus-1 enrichment. The Poisson integral on the disk is the harmonic counterpart of the Cauchy integral formula.

Partial differential equations

The Laplace equation is just one — the elliptic — vertex of the elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic triangle. Heat-equation regularity is parabolic Harnack; the Cauchy problem for the wave equation has no maximum principle. Harmonic theory is the test case for every weak-solution / regularity machine.

Probability and Brownian motion

A function $u$ is harmonic on a domain $\Omega$ iff $u(B_t)$ is a martingale until $B_t$ exits $\Omega$. Mean-value and maximum principles fall out of optional stopping; Dirichlet problems become Brownian-motion exit-distribution computations. Feynman–Kac is the conceptual peak.

Geometric analysis

Riemannian-manifold harmonic functions (defined by the Laplace–Beltrami operator) sit at the heart of Riemannian geometry; harmonic forms compute de Rham cohomology (Hodge theory); harmonic maps generalise to nonlinear targets and feed minimal-surface theory.

Open frontiers: non-divergence-form regularity (Krylov–Safonov), free-boundary problems (obstacle problem, Stefan), harmonic measure and the Wolff distortion theorem, and the Calderón–Zygmund machinery that turns harmonic theory into a singular-integral / $L^p$ theory of operators.
